kill_fact: No kill. The load-bearing catalyst survives independent primary-source verification: a DEFINITIVE signed agreement (announced 4/30/26) to sell Long Ridge Energy & Power to a MARA Holdings subsidiary for ~$1.52B, expected to close Q3 2026, is confirmed verbatim by BOTH FTAI's and MARA's own press releases. It is live, dated, binding, and NOT yet fired (regulatory approvals — FERC/HSR/STB — still pending as of June 2026; close is forward-dated to Q3). The spread is NOT closed: live price $4.81 (06-18-26) still sits below the $7.935 52-wk high and far below the $9.00 event-target. The PJM market-monitor risk is real but, as the dossier states, a CONDITIONING request (keep capacity in PJM markets), not opposition — MARA has stated it intends to keep selling into PJM, so it does not threaten FIP's fixed $1.52B consideration. CORRECTIONS NEEDED on two non-load-bearing facts (see below).
corrections: (1) NARRATIVE ERROR in bear_thesis/valley_of_death: the dossier claims 'the stock fell 7.4% on the Q1 print' AND frames the deal as fighting that drop — but primary sources show FIP GAINED ~+8.4% (peak +11.2%) on 4/30/26 when the Long Ridge sale was announced. The deal-day reaction was strongly POSITIVE, not a 7.4% drop; any 7.4% move belongs to a separate Q1-EPS session, not the catalyst. This does not change the asymmetry but the consensus_delta/positioning narrative should not imply the market punished the deal. (2) DEBT FIGURE: FTAI's own release specifies the deal 'immediately eliminate[s] $1.16B of Long Ridge debt' + '~$300M of parent debt' (~$1.46B gross) and assumed debt of 'at least $785M' per the MARA financing — consistent with the dossier's '~$1.4B removed,' but the precise split is $1.16B asset-level + $300M parent, not the dossier's '~$1.15B + ~$300M.' Minor. (3) FLOOR CAVEAT STANDS: floor is the EMPIRICAL $3.90 52-wk low, NOT a hard deal-break floor — FIP is the SELLER (no merger-arb downside protection from a target floor) with NEGATIVE book equity (-$303M) and a senior preferred stack ahead of common, so a deal-break re-exposes a thin, subordinated stub. The -19% floor is a price-history level, not a balance-sheet backstop.
evidence: FTAI Infrastructure IR press release, 2026-04-30: 'Announces Agreement to Sell Long Ridge Energy and Power to MARA Holdings, Inc.' — definitive agreement, ~$1.52B, eliminates $1.16B Long Ridge debt + ~$300M parent debt, expected close Q3 2026 subject to FERC/HSR/STB. (ir.fipinc.com / GlobeNewswire 3284709)

MARA Holdings IR press release, 2026-04-30: 'MARA Advances Its Optimized Digital Infrastructure Strategy with Agreement to Acquire Long Ridge Energy & Power' — confirms ~$1.5B incl. assumed debt, 505 MW CCGT Hannibal OH, close Q3 2026, customary regulatory approvals. (ir.mara.com /detail/1419)

Utility Dive / Monitoring Analytics filing Docket EC26-92, 2026-05-29: PJM Independent Market Monitor urges FERC to approve only if MARA commits not to remove Long Ridge capacity from PJM markets — a conditioning request (not opposition); MARA states it plans to keep selling into PJM. (utilitydive.com 821574; monitoringanalytics.com IMM_Comment_Docket_No_EC26-92_20260529.pdf)

stockanalysis.com/stocks/fip, 2026-06-18 close: live price $4.81, 52-wk range $3.90 low / $7.935 high, mkt cap ~$568M — confirms the live spread is open and the $3.90 floor / dossier price are accurate.
